channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 5 European nations say Alexei Navalny was poisoned and blame the Kremlin
item.description: In a joint statement, the foreign ministries of the U.K., France, Germany, Sweden and the Netherlands say Navalny was poisoned by Russia with a lethal...

item.title: California Billionaires Maneuver Now in Case Wealth Tax Passes
item.description: California’s wealthiest residents are maneuvering to reduce their net worth in case a billionaire tax becomes law. Some may even try to drop below $1 ...

item.title: Thousands Rally for Iran Regime Change in Cities Around the World
item.description: People protesting the Iranian government gathered near the security conference in Munich, as well as in other cities. More U.S.-Iran talks are expecte...

item.title: What We Know About the Kidnapping of Nancy Guthrie, Savannah Guthrie’s Mother
item.description: Nancy Guthrie, 84, the “Today” show anchor’s mother, vanished from her Arizona home on Feb. 1. In the time since, very little new information has come...

item.title: US military reports a series of airstrikes against Islamic State targets in Syria
item.description: The U.S. military says the strikes were carried out in retaliation of the December ambush that killed two U.S. soldiers and one American civilian inte...

item.title: Opinion: Disqualified but not forgotten
item.description: A Ukrainian athlete was disqualified from competition this week by the International Olympic Committee because his helmet had images of other Ukrainia...

item.title: It's a dangerous complication of pregnancy -- but a new drug holds promise
item.description: Researchers celebrate early results of a drug that may become the first treatment for a serious complication of pregnancy called preeclampsia. It's go...

item.title: American speedskater Jordan Stolz wins second Olympic gold with 500-meter race victory
item.description: With the win, Stolz joins Eric Heiden as the only skaters to take gold in both the 500 and 1,000 at the same Olympics.

item.title: Trump Erased the Endangerment Finding. Here Come the Lawsuits.
item.description: The battle is expected to reach the Supreme Court, which is far more conservative today than it was when the measure was established.

item.title: In Munich, Europe Says It Is ‘De-risking’ From the U.S. Amid Trump’s Unpredictability
item.description: In Munich, European leaders were also talking about “de-risking” from the United States, citing President Trump’s unpredictability.

item.title: Live Updates: In Munich, Rubio Calls Europe a Friend but Says It Must Change
item.description: European leaders expressed relief at the tone of Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s remarks, but they made it clear that the trans-Atlantic rift remaine...

item.title: Thousands Rally for Iran Regime Change in Cities Around the World
item.description: People protesting the Iranian government gathered near the security conference in Munich, as well as in other cities. More U.S.-Iran talks are expecte...

item.title: Europe’s Reaction to Rubio: Relief, Up to a Point
item.description: The secretary of state was much less caustic in Munich than Vice President JD Vance was a year ago. But European officials said his core message was m...

item.title: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen of Brazil and Mikhail Shaidorov of Kazakhstan Win Olympic Gold Medals
item.description: A Brazilian skier secured South America’s maiden medal at a Winter Games the day after a Kazakh figure skater won his nation’s first gold in 32 years.

item.title: How ICE Is Pushing Tech Companies to Identify Protesters
item.description: The DHS is flooding social media companies with administrative subpoenas to identify accounts that are protesting ICE. Social media companies have pus...
